intro: Alectorolophus × puberulus, also known as Chinese angelica or dong quai, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to China and used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to regulate menstrual cycles, alleviate symptoms of menopause, and improve cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Alectorolophus × puberulus include ferulic acid, apiol, and ligustilide, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, this plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures to promote women's health and well-being.
